title: Berlin Garden of Erotic Delights
authors: Erwin von Busse under the pseudonym "Granand"
description: Berlin Garden of Erotic Delights is a short story collection by Erwin von Busse under the pseudonym "Granand". Banned for "indecency" by courts in Berlin and Leipzig
descriptionParagraphs: Berlin Garden of Erotic Delights is a short story collection by Erwin von Busse under the pseudonym "Granand". Banned for "indecency" by courts in Berlin and Leipzig

Its interest lies partly in the way literary or informational writing gets collapsed into a public-morality problem. As a short story collection, it can be read not only for subject matter but for the way form, tone, and circulation make a text feel dangerous, intimate, or politically usable to anxious officials.

It also matters as part of a wider censorship history in Weimar Republic (1918-1933). The present page is a dossier starter built from source-tracked ban records; the surviving note currently says: Banned for "indecency" by courts in Berlin and Leipzig More publication history, translations, and close reading can be added later.

overview.englishDescription: Berlin Garden of Erotic Delights is a short story collection by Erwin von Busse under the pseudonym "Granand". Banned for "indecency" by courts in Berlin and Leipzig
banningParagraphs: Berlin Garden of Erotic Delights entered censorship debates as a short story collection associated with morality, print scandal, and sexuality. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around obscenity and public morality.

The earliest event currently captured here is Date not yet pinned down in Germany, where Weimar Republic (1918-1933) authorities banned publication or circulation. Banned for "indecency" by courts in Berlin and Leipzig Banned for "indecency" by courts in Berlin and Leipzig

counterReadings.title: Banned Books: Literature Suppressed on Sexual Grounds
counterReadings.author: Dawn B. Sova
counterReadings.note: Surveys the legal and moral language used to suppress books as obscene.

counterReadings.title: Banned Books: 387 B.C. to 1978 A.D.
counterReadings.author: Anne Lyon Haight
counterReadings.note: Useful for seeing how obscenity law and censorship habits changed over time.

counterReadings.title: 100 Banned Books: Censorship Histories of World Literature
counterReadings.author: Nicholas J. Karolides, Margaret Bald, and Dawn B. Sova
counterReadings.note: A compact reference on how censorship systems moved across states, churches, and courts.
